content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 6,
1995
Honorable Paul V. Kloecker
Mayor Pro Tern
City of Gilroy
8431 Delta Court
Gilroy, California
95020
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—95--0l8
Dear Mr. Kloecker:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding your
duties as under the conflict-of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
This letter confirms the
advice I provided to you during our telephone conversation.
2
1.
Conflicts of Interest Based on the Conflict of Interest
of A Participating Councilmember.
You have stated that you are a Councilmember for the City of
Gilroy and you do not have a financial interest in a decision
pending before the city council.
Nevertheless,
you are concerned
that you may have a conflict of interest if another councilmember
who has a conflict of interest participates in the decision.
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making,
or otherwise attempting to use his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
In addition,
as we discussed during our telephone conversation,
your letter raises issues concerning another public official’s
responsibilities under the Act.
Therefore, pursuant to Regulation
18329(b) (8) (B),
we decline to provide any advice to you regarding
that public official’s responsibilities under the Act.
In any
event, those issues already have been addressed in our Faber
Advice Letter,
No.
A-94-346.
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


the official has a financial interest.
3
Thus, this provision
prohibits a public official from participating in a governmental
decision if the official has a conflict of interest with respect
to that decision; it does not,
however, prohibit another
councilmember from participating in the decision.
Accordingly,
you do not have a conflict of interest if a councilmember who has
a conflict of interest in a decision participates in that
decision.
2.
Avoiding The Appearance of A Conflict of Interest.
You have inquired whether there is any requirement in the
Political Reform Act that a public official avoid the appearance
of a conflict of interest.
The Act does not include such a
requirement.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please call me at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Jeevan Ahuja
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:JA:ak
Regulations 18700 and 18700.1 define the terms “make,”
‘participate in making,” and “attempting to use his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision.”
Under
those definitions a public official who is disqualified from
participating in a decision, generally, is also prohibited from
participating in discussions concerning those decisions.

sections.question: You have stated that you are a Councilmember for the City of Gilroy and you do not have a financial interest in a decision pending before the city council. Nevertheless, you are concerned that you may have a conflict of interest if another councilmember who has a conflict of interest participates in the decision. You have inquired whether there is any requirement in the Political Reform Act that a public official avoid the appearance of a conflict of interest.
sections.conclusion: Thus, this provision prohibits a public official from participating in a governmental decision if the official has a conflict of interest with respect to that decision; it does not, however, prohibit another councilmember from participating in the decision. Accordingly, you do not have a conflict of interest if a councilmember who has a conflict of interest in a decision participates in that decision. The Act does not include such a requirement.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a councilmember have a conflict of interest if another councilmember with a conflict of interest participates in a decision, and does the Political Reform Act require officials to avoid the appearance of a conflict of interest?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A councilmember without a financial interest in a decision does not have a conflict of interest merely because another councilmember with a conflict of interest participates in that decision. The Political Reform Act does not require public officials to avoid the appearance of a conflict of interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes footnotes providing statutory references. Counsel notes in footnote 2 that another public official's responsibilities are declined as advisory subject matter. Clear question and conclusion sections present in letter format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have stated that you are a Councilmember for the City of Gilroy and you do not have a financial interest in a decision pending before the city council. Nevertheless, you are concerned that you may have a conflict of interest if another councilmember who has a conflict of interest participates in the decision. You have inquired whether there is any requirement in the Political Reform Act that a public official avoid the appearance of a conflict of interest.

CONCLUSION: Thus, this provision prohibits a public official from participating in a governmental decision if the official has a conflict of interest with respect to that decision; it does not, however, prohibit another councilmember from participating in the decision. Accordingly, you do not have a conflict of interest if a councilmember who has a conflict of interest in a decision participates in that decision. The Act does not include such a requirement.

Conflicts of Interest Based on the Conflict of Interest of A Participating Councilmember. Accordingly, you do not have a conflict of interest if a councilmember who has a conflict of interest in a decision participates in that decision. 2. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please call me at (916) 322—5901. Sincerely, Steven G. embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Mayor Pro Tem Kloecker's inquiry regarding whether he has a conflict of interest when another councilmember with a conflict participates in a city council decision, and whether the Political Reform Act requires avoiding the appearance of conflicts. The FPPC advises that Section 87100 only prohibits officials with financial interests from participating, not other officials, and that the Act contains no appearance-of-conflict requirement.
